title: Inclusion Rules
description: Inclusion rules are applied sequentially after the primary event to further filter the qualifying population. Each rule specifies a condition (e.g., age range, diagnosis, drug exposure) relative to the index date using temporal windows.
tips: Rules are AND-ed together — a patient must satisfy ALL rules to remain in the cohort.
Use negative temporal windows (e.g., -365 to -1) to require history before the index date.
The Attrition tab after generation shows exactly how many patients each rule removes.
An 'exit' rule (e.g., prior diagnosis) uses a negative window; an 'entry' rule uses a positive one.
Avoid very long windows without CDM coverage checks — patients with insufficient observation periods will be dropped automatically.
